description: SESSION CONTEXT LOADER: Fires when an agent session starts. Loads workspace context — DB connection status indicator, available agents, and a welcome banner — so the agent knows its operating environment immediately.

sessionStart.description: Inject project context into session on startup
sessionStart.powershell:
param($input)
$payload = $input | ConvertFrom-Json
$cwd = $payload.cwd
# Check if .env exists
$envExists = Test-Path (Join-Path $cwd '.env')
$envStatus = if ($envExists) { 'CONFIGURED' } else { 'MISSING — copy .env.example to .env' }
# Check if MCP server is built
$distExists = Test-Path (Join-Path $cwd 'mcp-server/dist/index.js')
$mcpStatus = if ($distExists) { 'BUILT' } else { 'NOT BUILT — run: cd mcp-server && npm run build' }
# Check output dir
$outputDir = Join-Path $cwd 'workspace/docs'
if (-not (Test-Path $outputDir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $outputDir | Out-Null }
$outputCount = (Get-ChildItem $outputDir -Filter '*.docx' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object).Count
# Build context output
$context = @"
=== ADF COPILOT AGENTS — SESSION STARTED ===
Project  : $cwd
.env     : $envStatus
MCP Build: $mcpStatus
Output/  : $outputCount existing document(s)
MEMORY   : $(if (Test-Path (Join-Path $cwd 'workspace/ADF_MEMORY.md')) { 'EXISTS (pipeline in progress)' } else { 'Not found (new session)' })

>>> ENTRY POINT: @adf-orchestrator <<<
  Start here for ALL new features. It coordinates the full pipeline.
  Type: @adf-orchestrator I need to build [your feature]

Pipeline Agents (called by orchestrator):
  @adf-requirements-agent — Step 1: Requirements + Word doc
  @adf-sql-agent          — Step 2: SQL inline queries
  @adf-builder-agent      — Step 3: Build ADF ARM template
  @adf-deploy-agent       — Step 4: Deploy to Azure ADF
  @adf-test-agent         — Step 5: Test + CSV + Word doc
  @adf-code-review-agent  — Step 6: Code review + Word doc

Active Safety Hooks:
  dry-run-enforcer    — Requires dryRun=true before real SQL execution
  audit-logger        — Logs all tool calls to logs/agent-audit.log
============================================
"@
# Write context to a temp file that the agent can read
$contextFile = Join-Path $cwd '.copilot-state/session-context.txt'
$stateDir = Join-Path $cwd '.copilot-state'
if (-not (Test-Path $stateDir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $stateDir | Out-Null }
Set-Content -Path $contextFile -Value $context
Write-Output $context
exit 0
